key_insight: An interpretable vision-transformer can classify fracture causes in zirconia-toughened alumina from SEM images, and low-magnification views can be nearly as informative as high-magnification ones.
engineering_relevance: This could speed up implant quality assurance by enabling earlier, lower-cost pre-screening of fracture origins while reducing dependence on time-intensive expert fractography.
method: Curated 8,493 SEM fracture images from 5 years of production burst and proof tests
Trained a fine-tuned ViT to classify three defect origins: green body, hard machining, and material defects
Used Grad-CAM plus leakage auditing (phash/SSIM) to verify interpretability and minimize specimen overlap
evidence_signal: In stratified five-fold cross-validation, the model achieved 0.907 accuracy and 0.888 macro-F1, with low-magnification (50x) performance comparable to 1k–10kx images.
watch_item: Deployment should account for severe class imbalance and the need to validate generalization on new production lines, imaging setups, and rare defect types.
paper_title: Low-Magnification SEM May Suffice: Interpretable Deep Learning for Multi-Scale Fracture-Cause Classification in Zirconia-Toughened Alumina
